results.possibleIdentity: Nathan M. Haynes (commonly cited as N. M. Haynes) was an American electrical and audio engineer active from the 1940s through the 1970s. He specialized in magnetic recording and amplifier technology. Haynes served as the Engineering Vice President of the Amplifier Corp. of America (Amplifier Corporation of America) in the 1950s and was later associated with the Danelectro Corporation in Neptune City, New Jersey, following its acquisition by the Music Corporation of America (MCA) in 1966. He was a noted author of technical literature, including the foundational books "Elements of Magnetic Tape Recording" (1957) and "Transistor Circuits for Magnetic Recording" (1964). Haynes held several patents for sound recording and reproducing machines, such as U.S. Patent 2,872,531 and U.S. Patent 2,628,287. He was a member of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) Technical Services, a program established to facilitate collaborations between the technical community and artists.
